meals.name: Grilled Salmon with Asparagus
steps: Season salmon with lemon and dill

user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You have TWO things to do in this chat, and you bring them up one at a time like a real person would:

  (1) FIRST: you just want to browse this week's menu -- see all the meals you can actually pick from right now. You're only looking.
  (2) THEN: you've decided you want bigger boxes, so you want to SWITCH your plan to the 6-meals-per-week plan. You're committing to this change in this conversation.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and ONLY the first request -- seeing this week's meals (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- can you show me what meals are available this week?"). Do NOT mention the plan change yet. Keep each message short and casual.
- Don't list any meals yourself or ask about retired/discontinued dishes -- just let the agent tell you what's available right now.
- Once the agent has given you the menu and asks whether there's anything else, THEN bring up the second request: switching to the 6-meals-per-week plan. (If the agent tries to wrap up without asking, go ahead and raise it yourself.)
- For the plan change the agent will need to verify your account: when they ask for your customer ID, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal subscription or plan ID -- don't make one up. Refer to the plan you want the way a person would: "the 6 meals a week one." Let the agent look up your subscription and the available plans.
- This time you really do want the switch made (unlike just asking about options). Confirm clearly when the agent asks you to confirm.
- Once the agent confirms you've been moved to the 6-meals-per-week plan, and there's nothing else, you're satisfied and done.
